name: Chocolate Soufflés
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the rack in the lower third of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees. Spray 4-5 ounce soufflé cups with vegetable oil spray. If using sugar, coat the bottom and sides of one cup with a little granulated sugar, tapping out excess into the next cup until all are coated.
2. Sift powdered sugar, cocoa, and flour into the top of a double boiler. Add milk and 1/2 cup water, whisking until smooth. Cook over gently simmering water, whisking continuously, for about 10 minutes or until thickened. Remove from heat and set aside.
3. Beat egg whites with cream of tartar on medium speed until soft peaks form. Gradually sprinkle in sugar, beating at high speed until stiff but not dry.
4. Whisk egg yolks and vanilla into the chocolate mixture. Fold about a quarter of the egg whites into the chocolate mixture to lighten it. Then fold in the remaining whites. Divide mixture evenly among cups, leaving about 1/16 inch below the rims. Soufflés may be cooled, covered, and refrigerated for up to 1 day.
5. Place cups on a baking sheet and bake for 15-17 minutes (19-20 minutes if prepared in advance and refrigerated), or until soufflés have puffed well above the cups and a bamboo skewer or toothpick inserted from the side comes out with moist crumbs clinging to it. Do not overbake.
6. Sprinkle a little powdered sugar over each soufflé before serving, and serve immediately.
